channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Outworld Station has launched into its 1.0 update
item.content:encoded:
Outworld Station has launched out of Early Access with its 1.0 update, bringing new features to Trickjump Games’ space-based base building and automation sim.

The goal, as with all automation games, is to build and grow your facility, synchronising internal supply and production chains to further continued expansion, and yet more optimisation. Outworld Station sets this in the void of space and the TAU system, allowing you to explore and find space debris that is then turned into resources, as well as linking to other stations and factories through wormholes to extend your reach even further.

The 1.0 update adds new missions, unlocks new station modules and technologies, and sees a new threat emerge on the horizon… the event horizon, I mean, as a giant and gorgeously rendered black hole dominates the new location of The Void and features in one of the two new storyline missions. If you’re closer to a planet, then the space elevator module lets you extract more high-value resources, while a new laboratory module offers up alternate recipes, and a teleporter enables to bounce around your stations more quickly.

Here’s the full breakdown of new features:

  • Storyline: Complete the Arkship and Escape The Void
  • New Location: The Void: Event Horizon
  • New Module: Space Elevator: Lift high-value resources from a planet’s surface
  • New Module: Laboratory: Research alternative and more efficient recipes for common items and parts
  • New Module: Recycler: Feed in unwanted resources in exchange for rewards
  • New Module: Teleporter: Fast travel between remote parts of your stations
  • New Module: Magnetic Accelerator Cannon
  • New Ships: Colossus Imperator Battlecruiser and Crusader Auxilliary Supply Ship
  • New Equipment: Antimatter Lazer
  • Overdrive Cores: Can be slotted into most production modules to significantly increase output
  • Steam Achievements: Lots of fun and challenging achievements to unlock
  • New Starting Locations when starting a new game
  • Can now Move existing buildings without selling or copying them
  • New items, materials and parts
  • Can now drag-out an area for tugs to drop completed ships at
  • Duplicate multiple buildings to create a one-off Blueprint
  • Production Overview now unlocks earlier
  • Production Overview now has an option to display precise values
  • Production Overview can now include input from Wormholes
  • Long TODO lists now scroll
  • Added game option to disable ship reactor meltdowns
  • Lots of bug fixes and performance improvements

Source: press release, Steam



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/06/outworld-station-has-launched-into-its-1-0-update/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Everything Is Crab Review – You are what you eat
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve ever wanted to live a version of your life where you spend a bunch of time just eating things you find on the ground and then randomly evolving, then good news, because Everything Is Crab: The Animal Evolution Roguelike is here to fill your very specific niche.

You’d be forgiven for thinking that Everything Is Crab is a Survivors-like, given the general presentation, but it’s not. There are no auto-attacks here, and it’s all timing and moving and dodging instead. Your aim is still to survive, but that’s about where the similarities end in terms of gameplay.

As you run around attacking creatures, they’ll turn into food for you to eat, and doing so earns you experience. Every time you level up you get to mutate slightly, with new attacks and new traits on offer. Some of these are things like health regen, some let you eat more food faster, and some work together to let you just obliterate a run with no issues. Go for elephant trunk, body slam, health and size boosts and you’re golden.

Just be careful what you pick, because some parts can exclude others – if you go for the antlers you won’t be able to get the horns, for example, or if you go for tentacles then you can kiss legs goodbye. It means that you’re constantly picking and fine-tuning your build as you go, and you get to choose different evolution paths, like becoming a shelled creature with wings, multiple eyes, and an alpha mentality. It’s all deeply silly, and seeing what you end up as is always a delight.

Each run lasts around 20 minutes, with multiple bosses to fight along the way along with different things to find to either fight off alpha enemies or choose from different upgrades. It means that it’s pretty easy to just hop into things and do a run before leaving your house to try and evolve a little bit yourself. The only thing I found a little irksome was that some of the runs end up feeling very similar despite the options available. I guess the name sort of hints at that.

There are a lot of different evolutions to choose from, both active and passive, but it all sort of coalesces and blends together across a lot of runs. While attacks might have different properties, many of the early ones are just “hit thing in a specific direction.” I wish there were a few more truly wild options to choose from sooner, as it can feel very samey after a fair few runs, especially if you can’t get a good build going.

Summary

Everything Is Crab is one of those games where you really get the whole game in the first run. If you like that first attempt, and the general vibe you're getting, then you're going to enjoy run after run. However, if you're hoping for more than what that first play session offers, you're going to find the game to be a little bit lacking. Still, it's a lot of fun if you're looking for a more pure arcade experience in your roguelike, and if you like seeing the horrifying monster your little blue blob can become once you've eaten half of a planet.

Good

  • Plenty of different choices and evolutions
  • Wonderfully cute art style

Bad

  • Runs can feel very samey
  • More variety in attacks would be great

Related stories from TSA



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/07/everything-is-crab-review-you-are-what-you-eat/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
